paper_title: A Real-Time Energy and Cost Efficient Vehicle Route Assignment Neural Recommender System

problem_definition.description: Vehicle assignment problems where n vehicles need to be deployed over m ≤ n trips
problem_definition.objective: Optimize for energy consumption and cost

system_architecture.name: POLARIS
system_architecture.description: Argonne-based high-performance, open-source agent-based modeling framework for simulating large-scale transportation systems

method.approach: Agent-based transportation modeling combined with high-fidelity vehicle system simulation
method.implementation: Models truck routes across Chicago and its suburbs under various scenarios
method.data_generation: Generates a large backbone dataset of vehicle-route energy outcomes

evaluation_metric.name: Total Cost of Ownership (TCO)
evaluation_metric.assumption: The lowest-cost powertrain option captures driving conditions and number of miles driven over the vehicle's lifetime

title: A Real-Time Energy and Cost Efficient Vehicle Route Assignment Neural Recommender System
